text: A uniformed league marks six shop doors with white paint while the watch records the vandalism as enthusiastic cartography.

choices.text: Guard the street with its residents

outcomes.text: Every marked door stays open. The league discovers a neighborhood is harder to terrorize after it learns everyone's name.
The watch removes the paint and leaves the painters' address book untouched.
By morning everyone knows who bought the paint, uniforms, and official silence.
effects: rumor 100 A wealthy merchant funded the uniformed league.

text: League recruiters reserve the back room for a meeting called Traditional Soup. The soup contains no tradition, only names and patrol routes.

choices.text: Warn every person on their list

outcomes.text: Doors lock, escorts form, and the patrol finds nobody isolated enough to frighten.
The league spends the night surrounding a laundry cart wearing a heroic blanket.
They pay in clean coins and dirty certainty. Someone else will pay the difference.

text: A marching column distributes maps where every poor district is labeled disorder and every wealthy estate is labeled civilization.

choices.text: Replace the maps with ownership records

outcomes.text: The column learns disorder has landlords, creditors, and a mailing address.
effects: rumor 95 The league's donors own the districts it calls disorder.

success.text: Travelers hold the line until the column discovers marching requires a road people consent to share.

fail.text: The blockade breaks. Its witnesses scatter with names, dates, and no intention of forgetting.

choices.text: Take a map and avoid notice

outcomes.text: You pass safely. The map marks your silence as open terrain.

text: Hidden beneath a ranger station is an archive of people once smuggled past an older uniformed league. Several rescuers later became respectable officials who denied everything.

outcomes.text: Families decide what becomes public. Gratitude and accusation occupy the same careful page.
effects: rumor 90 Survivors control the hidden rescue archive.

outcomes.text: The heroes receive statues. The survivors lose addresses they still needed hidden.
Respectability pays promptly when its memory is threatened.

text: Mountain guides can lead a hunted family across the pass tonight, but doing so will expose the only safe route used by dozens more.

choices.text: Move everyone and abandon the route

outcomes.text: The crossing takes all night. By dawn the trail is empty, the camp is ash, and everyone is alive.
They cross. The route survives for now, carrying the weight of those still waiting.
The route remains secret. So does what happens to the family below.

text: The league's founder lies preserved beneath the city, surrounded by donor ledgers and a machine waiting to broadcast his voice into every public square.

choices.text: Destroy the machine and publish the ledgers

outcomes.text: The dead voice stays dead. The living money finally has names.

success.text: Every square hears the founder thank bankers, landlords, and frightened officials by name.
effects: rumor 100 The league founder named the elites who funded him.

fail.text: The machine calls your interference proof of conspiracy and acquires a regrettable audience.

choices.text: Sell the ledgers to the donors

outcomes.text: They purchase their own signatures and finance another silence.

text: A veterans' hospital has more medals than bandages. The procurement office says honor was cheaper in bulk.

choices.text: Turn the parade budget into medicine

outcomes.text: The parade is canceled. Nobody in the ward misses being thanked by a horse.
Collectors pay more for untouched heroism than the state paid for damaged bodies.
A few veterans receive care beneath a plaque naming the donor instead of the war.

text: Munitions workers meet after closing. They can stop the shell line tomorrow, but the factory is the town's only wage and the front is already hungry for ammunition.

choices.text: Back the strike and build a relief kitchen

outcomes.text: The shell line stops. Soup begins. The town learns survival and obedience were never the same job.
Fewer shells leave. Workers keep wages and acquire the exhausted secrecy of partial refusal.
The bounty buys dinner. The factory buys locks. The front receives everything on schedule.

text: A conscription wagon blocks the road, collecting farmhands while their harvest rots behind them and officers call both losses necessary.

success.text: The wagon leaves empty. The fields gain hands and the army gains a story it cannot arrest.

fail.text: Several escape while you become the wagon's least cooperative passenger.

outcomes.text: An entire village develops a rare allergy to imperial borders.
The road clears. The fields remain full of food nobody is there to gather.

text: Trees have grown through an abandoned battlefield, lifting rusted swords above the soil like questions nobody in uniform answered.

outcomes.text: The monument lists cooks, children, porters, prisoners, and villages described in dispatches only as terrain.
effects: rumor 90 The forest memorial names the civilians erased from the war record.

choices.text: Melt the weapons into farm tools

outcomes.text: Steel returns to the soil with a less ambitious job.
The cleanest sword fetches the best price. Its rust was apparently too historical.

text: Two warships order your vessel to identify the enemy. Each captain points at the other and neither notices the fishing boats between them.

outcomes.text: The civilians escape while both navies accuse you of depriving them of strategic scenery.

success.text: Confused officers begin exchanging prisoners before anyone corrects the grammar.

fail.text: Both fleets agree you are the enemy, achieving the day's only diplomatic breakthrough.

outcomes.text: The coordinates are accurate. Your conscience requests a less precise profession.

text: The buried command engine can end the war by destroying every army, including conscripts, medics, prisoners, and the villages where soldiers sleep.

outcomes.text: The war continues without an easy apocalypse. People remain responsible for stopping it themselves.

choices.text: Target only the generals' command network

success.text: Orders vanish. Soldiers discover the front cannot explain why it exists without distant voices.
effects: rumor 95 Both armies have lost their command network.

fail.text: The engine mistakes rank for hat height and removes several innocent helmets explosively.

choices.text: Fire it for the highest bidder

outcomes.text: One army disappears. The buyer immediately discovers an appetite for the word next.

text: A schoolhouse shelters families whose language has been outlawed across the border. Officials offer safety if the children accept new names.

choices.text: Register the school under its own language

outcomes.text: Lessons continue. Every spoken name becomes both ordinary and defiant.

choices.text: Accept false names for immediate safety

outcomes.text: The children remain alive and learn to carry two names, one for documents and one for home.

choices.text: Move the families beyond city records

outcomes.text: They disappear from official danger and from every public claim to belonging.

text: {witness} asks the room to record names from a destroyed village. A merchant objects that testimony may harm regional confidence.

choices.text: Write every name into the Chronicle

outcomes.text: The list outlasts the merchant's confidence and the officials who preferred a smaller number.
effects: rumor 100 A survivor has named the people erased from a destroyed village.

choices.text: Record only what can be verified

outcomes.text: The careful list is defensible and incomplete. Missing names remain missing for reasons accuracy cannot repair.

choices.text: Ask for a more convenient evening

outcomes.text: The room returns to dinner. {witness} returns to being the only archive present.

text: A deportation convoy carries families toward a border post whose return orders use the phrase final processing without defining it.

choices.text: Disable the convoy and open the wagons

success.text: The locks break. Families scatter toward prepared shelters while guards discover paperwork cannot pursue in every direction.

fail.text: One wagon opens before the guards stop you. Its passengers carry the route to the others.

outcomes.text: The convoy turns around because officials fear illness more reliably than atrocity.
You preserve proof. The convoy continues, making evidence feel necessary and unbearable.
effects: rumor 100 A deportation convoy was sent toward a documented killing site.

text: Wetland reeds grow through a buried archive of confiscated family records. The occupying government called the people vanished and kept receipts for their houses.

outcomes.text: Ownership, kinship, and grief return in bundles tied with wet string.

choices.text: Preserve copies in a public archive

outcomes.text: Denial becomes harder, though never embarrassed enough to disappear.
effects: rumor 100 Confiscation records prove the vanished families were systematically dispossessed.

outcomes.text: The theft changes handwriting and continues.

text: A monastery can hide fifty refugees through winter. Two hundred are climbing toward it and the grain store cannot be argued into becoming larger.

choices.text: Open the doors and ration together

outcomes.text: Hunger enters with everyone else. Nobody is selected for danger by a full stomach.
The choice saves many and divides families according to a logic nobody calls just.

choices.text: Lead the rest toward another pass

success.text: The second route holds. Two shelters survive because one person refused the arithmetic offered.

fail.text: The storm takes the trail. Survivors reach the monastery carrying fewer hands than they began with.

text: The sealed ministry vault contains orders, transport lists, and signed reports proving the extermination campaign was planned. Outside, survivors wait to decide what justice can still mean.

choices.text: Give the archive to the survivors' assembly

outcomes.text: Those targeted control the evidence and the terms of its release. History stops being custody held by the state that made it.
effects: rumor 100 Survivors now hold the ministry archive proving the extermination campaign.

outcomes.text: The proof spreads beyond seizure. Private names, hiding places, and unfinished grief spread with it.

choices.text: Trade the archive for safe passage

outcomes.text: Some officials flee, some survivors live, and the bargain joins the archive as another fact nobody can make clean.

text: The museum displays ceremonial masks beside a plaque thanking the expedition that took them during a fire the expedition started.

outcomes.text: The empty cases remain with new plaques explaining why emptiness is more honest than possession.
The museum offers weekends and retains the word custody, which reveals the entire problem efficiently.
You repeat the expedition at a smaller scale and with worse handwriting.

text: A retired governor tells a charming story about discovering a valley whose residents served him dinner on the first night.

choices.text: Ask who cooked the dinner and owned the valley

outcomes.text: The charming story develops people, names, taxes, troops, and an ending the governor had misplaced.
effects: rumor 90 The governor's discovery story concealed an occupied community.

choices.text: Let the room enjoy the story

outcomes.text: Applause preserves the valley exactly as he remembers it: empty except for servants.

choices.text: Sell signed copies of his memoir

outcomes.text: Nostalgia moves briskly when the people excluded from it cannot afford a table.

text: A new border cuts a grazing route in half. Soldiers demand passports from herders whose grandparents named every spring on both sides.

outcomes.text: The crossing becomes too large to arrest and too ordinary to describe as invasion.
The route survives as a privilege renewed by people who invented its interruption.
The herds cross farther uphill. The border learns geography has not signed the agreement.

text: A crown survey labels the forest vacant because its residents rotate fields, share paths, and failed to build a palace visible from the surveyor's horse.

outcomes.text: The map loses its confidence. The forest keeps every path the paper ignored.

choices.text: Document how the land is used

outcomes.text: The residents produce a map of food, graves, stories, water, and obligations no crown ledger can price.
Your empty parcel contains a garden, a shrine, and several people unimpressed by paperwork.

text: An old fort has become a hotel whose luxury rooms were once cells. Descendants of prisoners are hired to perform cheerful sieges for guests.

choices.text: Turn the fort into a public memorial

outcomes.text: The cells keep their doors open. Visitors pay nothing and leave with fewer comfortable verbs.
The work becomes less exploitative without becoming less strange.
The pillow menu includes denial, nostalgia, and a smaller denial for children.

text: The conqueror's vault contains treaty stones broken after signing. Each fragment still repeats the promises made before the land was taken.

choices.text: Return the stones to the signatories' heirs

outcomes.text: The fragments become evidence, memorial, and a demand that outlived every official seal.

choices.text: Reassemble them for the royal court

outcomes.text: The court recognizes the treaty as historically important and currently inconvenient.
Collectors display broken promises behind excellent glass.

text: The city installs armored guards outside the public clinic to prevent desperate people from entering desperately.

outcomes.text: The line gets shorter because people receive care instead of instructions about where not to stand.
The shields gain padded edges. The locked door remains impressively humane.
Order improves for anyone whose illness has disposable income.

text: Off-duty watch officers occupy half the tavern and demand free drinks as compensation for protecting it from the people already inside.

outcomes.text: Every mug disappears behind the bar. Authority discovers thirst has no badge exemption.

choices.text: Buy one round to keep peace

outcomes.text: Peace lasts exactly one round and sends you the bill.
The officers become polite after learning memory has witnesses.
effects: rumor 90 Tavern staff documented watch extortion.

text: A checkpoint records every traveler's destination, employer, relatives, reading habits, and opinion of checkpoints.

choices.text: Jam the registry with invented citizens

outcomes.text: Ten thousand imaginary cousins overwhelm the archive and all report suspicious interest in privacy.
The line stops moving until the questions do. Collective delay defeats individual suspicion.
You pass and receive a receipt proving the state now knows where to find your honesty.

text: A sunken prison ledger lists people held without charge until the swamp reclaimed the walls and released everyone through structural criticism.

choices.text: Return the names to their families

outcomes.text: Absence gains dates, signatures, and officials who can no longer call it rumor.
effects: rumor 100 The prison ledger proves people were held without charge.

choices.text: Build a memorial from the cell doors

outcomes.text: Every open door bears a name. The memorial cannot be entered without passing through release.

choices.text: Sell the ledger back to the ministry

outcomes.text: The ministry pays to restore uncertainty.

text: The border garrison offers medicine to an isolated village if residents report every stranger who crosses the pass.

outcomes.text: Supplies arrive by goat, rope, and people who understand care should not recruit informants.

choices.text: Accept and report only harmless strangers

outcomes.text: The village gets medicine and learns to manufacture harmlessness on forms.

choices.text: Take the medicine and report nobody

outcomes.text: The first shipment arrives. The second consists entirely of questions.

text: A buried census engine can predict dissent by measuring hunger, rent, friendship, and how often a person looks at a guard without gratitude.

choices.text: Destroy the engine and publish its criteria

outcomes.text: The machine dies. Everyone learns the state had renamed suffering as evidence against sufferers.

choices.text: Use it to warn targeted people

outcomes.text: Many escape. The machine remains, and every warning depends on someone continuing to control it.
Your accuracy improves as your neighbors disappear.
